content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Alice Ivee 
341 S. Whipple street 
Fort Bragg, CA 
95437 
Dear Ms. Ivec: 
July 30, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-183 
You have requested advice on behalf of Mendocino County 
supervisor John Cimolino regarding the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act.lI 
This letter will confirm 
the telephone advice I provided to you on July 21, 1987. 
QUESTIONS 
How are donations received to augment a county supervisor's 
travel budget to be reported? 
How are payments made from such funds 
to be reported? 
CONCLUSIONS 
Donations received for travel in connection with a county 
supervisor's official duties must be reported as contributions under 
the campaign disclosure provisions of the Act. 
Payments made from 
such funds must be reported as expenditures. 
FACTS 
You are the campaign treasurer for Mendocino County supervisor 
John Cirnolino. 
Mr. Cimolino would like to set up a fund to receive 
donations and make payments to replace travel funds which have been 
eliminated from the county budget. 
Your letter states that the 
funds will be used only for travel in connection with Mr. Cimolino's 
official duties. 
ANALYSIS 
The act requires all donations received by an elected 
officeholder to be disclosed as either gifts (Sections 87200--872 0) 
or as contributions (Sections 84200-84217). 
By regulation, the 
lIGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code 
section 18000, et 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the Californ 
Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


Alice Ivec 
Page 2 
commission has interpreted the term "contribution" to include a 
payment received by or made at the behest of: 
... A candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding 
circumstances that the payment was received or made at his or 
her behest for personal purposes unrelated to his or her 
candidacy or status as an officeholder. 
(Regulation 18215(a) (2) (A), 
copy enclosed.) 
In an opinion issued to the Mayor Janet Gray Hayes Dinner 
Committee, 1 FPPC Opinions 210 (No. 75-145, December 4, 1975, copy 
enclosed), the Commission ruled that funds received by an elected 
official's campaign committee which are expended for activities 
related to the officeholder's responsibilities as an elected 
official and future candidate are properly treated as campaign 
expenditures. 
If, however, the funds are used for personal travel 
expenses, they are a gift to the official and must be reported on 
his or her statement of economic interests (Form 721) if funds 
aggregating $50 in a calendar year are received from a single source 
(Sections 82030, 87203, 87207). 
with regard to reporting donations received for official travel 
expenses, you may use Mr. Cimolino's current campaign committee, if 
any, to report the donations received and payments made. 
Any new 
committee formed for the purpose of receiving such funds will be a 
"controlled commitee" as defined in Section 82016 and the 
committee's disclosure reports must be filed at the same time(s) and 
in the same place(s) as Mr. Cimolino is required to file his regular 
campaign disclosure reports (Sections 84200-842i7). 
Enclosed is a copy of the 1987 "Information Manual on Campaign 
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act" to assist you in 
determining the committee's filing obligations. 
Please do not 
hesitate to contact me if you have additional questions. 
DMG: CW: 1m 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By: 
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 


7 
Fair Politicol Practices Commission 
428 IIJII Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Ms. Griffith: 
341 S. Whipple Street 
Fort Bragg, CA 95437 
June 16, 1987 
On Monday, June 15, 1987, I spoke to a member of the 
Commission, regarding the procedure of setting up a 
committee that an elected official could use funds 
from for travel connected with his office. 
I was told 
that we need to file a form 410 with the Secretary of 
State, obtain an identification number such as a person 
obtains when they run for office. 
Also the same guide 
lines would apply to this committee, such as filing dates, 
etc. 
The reason for the committee is to augment travel 
funds that have been cut from county budgets. 
I am requesting an opinion regarding this matter, in 
writing. 
You may contact me at the above address or 
by telephone at 707-964-4081 work, or 707-964-2313 after 
5:00 P.M. 
It is my understanding that we would report funds 
and expenditures just as a candidate does when they 
run for an elected position. 
Thank you for any assistance you may be able to give 
me on this matter. 
Alice Ivec
